title: Orchestration Primitive — routing.json
description: Per-slot routing table produced by the bake-off pipeline (data/routing.json), consumed at runtime to pick a model per task. Scores are 0-100.

_README.description: In-band human documentation. Consumers should ignore it (skip it when iterating slot keys).

model.description: Provider/model id (e.g. 'ollama/qwen3:8b', 'anthropic/claude-opus-4-7'), OR a sentinel: 'TO_BE_BAKED' (pre-bake-off; the runtime consuming this table should fall through to its default) or 'queue-for-human' (frontier-only slot; the consuming runtime should queue the call for a human).

fallback_model.description: Model the consuming runtime should fall back to if the primary errors.

judge_model.description: Identifier of the judge that produced this entry's scores.

last_baked_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the bake-off that wrote this entry. null until first bake-off. A 'queue-for-human' slot with a non-null last_baked_at means it WAS measured and the verdict was 'no OSS pick' — that still counts as baked for the completeness gate.

p50_quality_score.description: Median quality score (0-100) of the winning model on the bake-off scenario set.

p50_latency_ms.description: Median wall-clock latency in ms observed during bake-off.

quality_pct_of_judge.description: Winning model's quality as a percentage of the frontier judge baseline. The load-bearing delegation metric.

cost_per_call_usd.description: Mean cost per call. 0 for local Ollama models.

rationale.description: Human-readable explanation of the pick + known failure modes.
